_comment: Generic example DVT — exercises every feature of the generator (interpolation, cross-refs, external refs, lint). Copy this file, rename, and edit for a new product. The schema is also documented in dvt_data_template.json.

meta.title: DESIGN VERIFICATION TEST
meta.subtitle: Sub-Assembly Boundary — Functional and Stress Verification
meta.descriptor: Generic example DVT — copy this file and edit meta + content to spin up a real product DVT
meta.creator: Engineering

coverRows.label: Unit Under Test (UUT)
coverRows.value: Example Device — PN XXXXXX, Rev A (replace with real description)

coverRows.label: Test Bench
coverRows.value: Example bench / fixture name and key instruments

coverRows.label: Release Gate
coverRows.value: Section severity → required sections per Appendix B. Section A (external Sub-Assembly DVT) must be signed off before this DVT begins.

coverRows.label: Sibling Documents
coverRows.value: Sub-Assembly DVT (PCB-level, see Section A); other related DVTs and reports

coverRows.label: Format
coverRows.value: Contents page (clickable TOC) immediately after cover. Section A = external reference. Sections B onward = table-based per-test rows. Tester records measurement in Measure column. Field refresh (Word: Ctrl+A → F9; LibreOffice: Tools → Update → Fields) updates page numbers and TOC P/F mirror.

knownIssues.heading: Known Issues
knownIssues.intro: Suite-side xfail entries and corresponding DVT step pass criteria. Each row remains until fix releases and XPASSes.

externalRefs.title: Sub-Assembly DVT
externalRefs.intro: Sub-assembly qualification (PCB-level functional, AOI) is owned by the external Sub-Assembly DVT. This document begins at the assembled-unit boundary; bare-board coverage is not repeated here.
externalRefs.passCriteria: Signed Sub-Assembly DVT on record for the sub-assembly serial number installed in this unit. Signed copy attached as artifact. Section B does not begin until Section A artifact is present.

appendixA.heading: Appendix A — Equipment
appendixA.intro: All test equipment, fixtures, and instrumentation referenced from test rows.

appendixB.heading: Appendix B — Release-Gate Matrix
appendixB.intro: Change severity mapping to required DVT sections. Refine with stakeholders before treated as authoritative.

appendixC.heading: Appendix C — Elaborations and Rationale
appendixC.intro: Context and rationale referenced by Brief column. Listed by ref or topic.

sections.name: Mechanical / Pre-energize Checks
sections.intro: Run de-energized before mounting in the test fixture. Catches assembly defects that destroy hardware or invalidate downstream measurements at first power-up.

sections.name: Functional Bench Tests
sections.intro: Exercise documented interfaces with UUT in the test fixture. Tolerances trace to the product specification.

sections.name: Stress and Environmental
sections.intro: Power-cycle and soak. Catches drift-class defects that single-shot tests miss.

sections.name: Integration Cross-Checks
sections.intro: Cross-checks at system level. Run only after Sections [mech]–[stress] pass on bench.

steps.brief: Inspect enclosure, harness, fastener seating with light and loupe.
steps.criteria: No visible damage; all fasteners at spec torque; cable ties tight

steps.brief: Tug-test every external connector and internal harness mate.
steps.criteria: All seated; latches engaged; tug-test holds; no connector damage

steps.brief: Apply 500 VDC line-to-chassis for 60 s with signal cables disconnected.
Apply nominal supply; capture boot banner over console.
steps.criteria: Banner ≤ 2 s; identity string matches expected build; zero framing errors over 60 s idle

steps.brief: DMM-measure each rail at nominal supply with UUT idle.
steps.criteria: Each rail within spec; idle current ≤ documented max

steps.brief: Sweep supply ±10% in 1 V steps with 30 s dwell. Log rails each step.
steps.criteria: Rails within [func.1.2] tolerances at every step; no brownout, reset, or comm drop

steps.brief: Read identity registers via primary comm interface at default address.
steps.criteria: Identity values match expected; zero frame/parity/CRC errors over 5 min

steps.brief: Continuous polling at production rate for 60 s. Repeat [func.2.1] under load.
steps.criteria: Zero exceptions, retries, or framing errors; identity reads stable

steps.brief: 50 power cycles at nominal dwell. Capture boot and key telemetry each cycle.
steps.criteria: All 50 boot clean; no fault accumulation; rails within [func.1.2]

steps.brief: 24 h continuous operation under representative load. Log rails + ambient temp.
steps.criteria: No drift; no resets; bounded temp; logs complete; zero unexpected faults

steps.brief: Sub-tests under live load with companion subsystems connected.
steps.criteria: Each sub-scenario produces expected behavior per the system test plan; no regressions vs prior release

knownIssues.defect: Example: known firmware defect that masks XYZ behavior on Rev A units.
Example: interface contract change waiting on platform-side update.

elaborations.text: Section [mech] runs de-energized to catch assembly defects that would destroy hardware or invalidate downstream electrical measurements. Mirrors the assembled-unit-level safety check from the sub-assembly DVT.
Continuous polling at production rate surfaces drift defects that single-read tests miss. Mark XFAIL in known-issues only when a defect is documented and not release-blocking.

24 h soak is the regression guard for any timing or buffer-management defect that only surfaces under sustained activity. Attach the runner's summary, first/last iteration logs, and rail traces to the data sheet.

Appendix B is a starting point. Refine the change-severity categories and the compatibility matrix with Manufacturing Engineering, Quality, and the system-DVT owner before treating it as authoritative.

equipment.name: Programmable DC supply
equipment.action: UUT supply for nominal and sweep ([func.1.3])

categories.name: Firmware feature with interface contract change
categories.required: Automated suite + full [func] + diff against prior release

categories.name: Hardware change at sub-assembly level
categories.required: External Sub-Assembly DVT (Section A) re-signed + full Section [func] + [stress] + [integration]

categories.name: Combined hardware + firmware release
categories.required: External Sub-Assembly DVT (Section A) re-signed + full DVT (B through E) + system-DVT regression

cells: Next-generation variant. Add row when board enters DVT.
